title: Handwriting Test for Preschool Children (HT-PRE)
author: Qin Hong
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.5fmg3k6

steps.instruction: Before the test, parents completed the "Children's Basic Situation Questionnaire" so that necessary information about the participants could be gathered, including the child's age, gender, medical history, and trauma history.
The HT-PRE was then conducted one-on-one.
Write the most familiar contents, including name, age, class, Arabic number (1-9) in sequence by memory.
“The Individual Record Form” was used to record all special behavior observed during testing.
Write 10 two-digit numbers the numbers, which is randomly selected in the random number table by listening.
24 alphabet letters (except O and I which are easily to be confused with the numbers), which are randomly arranged into two lines, are required to be copied sequentially.

12 Chinese characters contained all the basic strokes and structures were selected from the 1st to the 4th volume of the primary school textbooks, and were requested to be copied in the same blank standard character grid.
